key_classes: ContentCreate - Input schema with validation (min_length, max_length)
ContentUpdate - Optional fields for partial updates
ContentResponse - Full output with ORM mapping
ContentListResponse - Paginated list with total count
ContentListParams - Filter parameters for list operations
file_insights.validation_patterns: Strict field constraints (title: 1-500 chars, channels as list), default factories for collections, optional AI model tracking

key_classes: GenerationRequest - Model selection with prompt and constraints
GenerationResponse - Model output with token tracking
file_insights.validation_patterns: Token bounds (1-16384), model key abstraction, optional system prompt support, streaming-ready chunk structure

key_classes: CitationResponse - Nested structure for source references
ValidationResponse - Scored validation with citations and raw results
file_insights.validation_patterns: Score range enforcement (0.0-1.0), nested model composition, provider abstraction, version tracking for audit

patterns_discovered.description: All response schemas include `model_config = {"from_attributes": True}` enabling direct mapping from SQLAlchemy ORM objects
patterns_discovered.benefit: Seamless object-to-schema conversion without manual field mapping

patterns_discovered.description: Separate schemas for input (ContentCreate), modification (ContentUpdate), and output (ContentResponse) following REST best practices
patterns_discovered.benefit: Clear API contracts, prevents ID/timestamp injection in requests, explicit immutable fields in responses

patterns_discovered.description: Schemas reference ContentStatus and ContentType enums from models.content, establishing single source of truth
patterns_discovered.benefit: Type safety, validation at schema level, prevents invalid state values

patterns_discovered.description: Consistent use of Pydantic Field() with constraints: min_length, max_length, ge/le for ranges
patterns_discovered.benefit: Declarative validation, automatic error messages, API documentation generation

patterns_discovered.description: CitationResponse nested within ValidationResponse for complex data structures
Use of `Field(default_factory=list)` for mutable defaults in ContentCreate
patterns_discovered.benefit: Prevents mutable default gotcha, ensures each instance has independent list

gotchas_discovered.description: No exception/error response schemas defined (e.g., ValidationError, NotFound). API will need consistent error models for documentation and client handling.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Clients must infer error response structure; OpenAPI/Swagger docs incomplete for error cases

gotchas_discovered.description: ContentListParams class appears to be a query schema but has no corresponding response wrapper. Likely needs to map to ContentListResponse.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Unclear how filter parameters flow through the system; may indicate incomplete endpoint implementation

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Generation Schemas May Have Incomplete Round-Trip
gotchas_discovered.description: GenerationRequest imports ContentType but GenerationResponse doesn't include content_type. Relationship between input type and output unclear.
gotchas_discovered.impact: API clients may struggle to correlate requests with responses; missing audit trail

gotchas_discovered.description: Repeated `model_config = {"from_attributes": True}` across multiple schemas. Could be centralized in a base class.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Code duplication; maintenance burden if config needs to change across all response models

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: No Explicit Field Examples or Descriptions
gotchas_discovered.description: Most Field() definitions lack description= and examples= parameters. GenerationRequest.model has description but others don't.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Auto-generated API docs lack useful context; harder for API consumers to understand field purposes

approach_outcome.summary: Comprehensive schema layer successfully created with three well-structured modules covering content operations, AI generation, and validation. Schemas follow Pydantic best practices and establish clear API contracts.
deliverables_met: ✓ Content endpoints schemas (Create, Update, List, StatusTransition, Response, Versions)
✓ Validation request/response schemas with citation tracking
✓ Generation request/response schemas with model abstraction
✓ All schemas include proper validation constraints
approach_outcome.quality_assessment: High - Code is clean, consistent, and follows Pydantic conventions. Proper use of Optional types, Field constraints, and nested models.
approach_outcome.integration_readiness: Ready for endpoint implementation - schemas provide solid foundation for route handlers

recommendations.recommendation: Create base response schema classes to eliminate model_config repetition
recommendations.rationale: DRY principle; single point to manage ORM mapping configuration
recommendations.example: Create BaseResponseSchema(BaseModel) with model_config, inherit in response schemas

recommendations.recommendation: Add explicit error response schemas (BadRequest, ValidationError, NotFound, etc.)
recommendations.rationale: Complete API contract definition; enables auto-generation of error documentation in OpenAPI/Swagger
recommendations.example: Create schemas/errors.py with ErrorDetail, ValidationErrorResponse, etc.

recommendations.recommendation: Add Field descriptions and examples to all schema fields
recommendations.rationale: Improves auto-generated API documentation; helps API consumers understand field purposes
recommendations.example: title: str = Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=500, description='Content headline', example='Breaking News')

recommendations.recommendation: Ensure GenerationResponse includes content_type and correlates with GenerationRequest
recommendations.rationale: Enables round-trip tracing and audit logs; clarifies request-response relationship
recommendations.example: Add content_type: ContentType field to GenerationResponse

recommendations.recommendation: Create pydantic schema tests validating edge cases (boundary values, invalid enums, missing required fields)
recommendations.rationale: Ensures schema validation works as intended; catches issues before they reach endpoint handlers
recommendations.example: Tests for title min/max length boundaries, token_used range validation

recommendations.recommendation: Consider adding __repr__ or json_schema_extra for better debugging and API documentation
recommendations.rationale: Improves developer experience; makes generated docs more discoverable
